title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Fast fashion refers to the rapid production of inexpensive clothing that (0) up with the latest trends. While this business model has made fashionable clothes more (1) to consumers, it has also created serious environmental problems. The fashion industry is now responsible (2) approximately ten percent of global carbon emissions. (3) the production of synthetic fabrics releases harmful microplastics into our oceans. Many fast fashion items are worn only a few times before being (4) away, contributing to growing mountains of textile waste. In recent years, consumers have become increasingly (5) of these issues. Some shoppers are now choosing to buy second-hand clothing or to (6) for more sustainable brands. Fashion companies are also (7) pressure to change their practices. Several major retailers have (8) initiatives to recycle old garments and reduce their environmental footprint. However, critics argue that these efforts are merely a drop in the ocean compared to what is truly needed.

example.explanation: 'Keeps up with' is the correct phrasal verb for keeping up with something.

questions.explanation: 'Accessible to' is the correct collocation to indicate that something is within someone's reach.
'Responsible for' is the correct preposition after 'responsible'.
'Moreover' adds additional information that reinforces the previous argument.
'Thrown away' is the most common phrasal verb for throwing something away.
'Aware of' is the correct expression to indicate awareness of a problem.
'Opt for' is the correct phrasal verb to choose one option among several.
'Under pressure' is the fixed expression that means to be pressed.
'Launched initiatives' is the correct placement to launch programs or campaigns.
